titleStmt.title: SII 25.107: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 107. (A.R. No. 107 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.
This record may be assigned to 12(13th century on palaeographical grounds.

The record states that a certain Śaṅkaranārāyaṇaṉ Tiruvāṇḍāḷ, the wife of Mōri Vishṇu Vishṇu, son of the kiḻār of Arugandūr, a hamlet of Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya in Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu and her younger sister Kuḻali deposited 56 śōḻiya-naṟ-paḻaṅ-kāśu to redeem some dēvadāna lands from Maruṅgūr of the deity that had been probably pledged to the mūlaparishai-mahāsabhai and to assign them in lieu of the said value for the purpose of daily food offerings to the goddess paḷḷi-aṟaināchchiyār of the temple of Tiruttaḷiyāṇḍār. It is mentioned that the rice supplied for the food offerings is measured by śūla-nāḻi.

The Parākrama Pāṇḍya of this record may be identified with Māṟavarmaṉ Parākrama-Pāṇḍya of accession 1143 A.D. with the praśasti Tirumagaḷ Puṇara, etc., whose 12th year record is found at Tirupputtūr (No. 131 of 1908).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
§ svastiśrī . tribhuvanaccakravattikaḷ śrīparākrama pāṇṭiya devaṟku yāṇṭu 10 6 vati netirāmāṇṭu keraḷaciṅkavaḷanāṭṭu brahma
deya ntiruppattū rarukan=tūr kiḻār makaḷ moriviṣṇa viṣṇaviṉ brāhmaṇi caṅkara nārāyaṇaṉ tiruvāṇṭāḷu mivaṭaṅkai
kuḻaliya miviruvom tiruttaliyāṇṭār tiruppaḷḷi Aṟainācciyāṟku nittañ cūlanāḻiyā lirunāḻi tiruvaḻutarici
yu mitaṟku veṇṭum vyaṉjanaṅkaḷu candrādityavaṟ celvatāka Ittevatevatānam maruṅkūrvayal nāḷutikkum veṉṟāṉuḷ
ḷiṭṭa nilaṅkaḷoṟṟi cora Oṭukkiṉa coḻiya naṟpaḻaṅ kācu 8 10 6 Eṇpattāṟukkum Ivūr vaṭavāy vayaṟ pacitāṅki Erikkiḻite
var tevatānam karuṅkuḻi . Iddharmma celvatāka kalveṭṭik kuṭuttom mulavakai mahāsabhaiyom . Itu śrīmāheśva
rarakṣai
Digital edition of SII 25.107 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
